name: Frequency Expressions — 常常, 有时候, 从来不

cards.grammarNote: Frequency adverbs go before the verb in Chinese, usually after the subject. They cannot go after the verb the way some English adverbs can.
从来不 = have never / never do (habitual). 从来没有 = have never done (completed). Both go before the verb.

cards.front: I exercise three times a week.
cards.romanized: Wǒ měi zhōu yùndòng sān cì.
cards.grammarNote: Use 次 (cì) after a number to count how many times. Place the frequency phrase (number + 次) after the verb.

cards.front: Could you take a quick look for me?
cards.romanized: Nǐ néng bāng wǒ kàn yīxià ma?
cards.grammarNote: V + 一下 (yīxià) = do briefly / casually, or softens a request. 一下 makes the action sound lighter and less demanding.
